title: Christian Pulisic ruled out for USMNT's World Cup match against Australia
original_text:
Christian Pulisic ruled out for USMNT's World Cup match against Australia
The U.S. men's national team has officially submitted its 2026 World Cup roster to FIFA — with injured center back Chris Richards as a part of the squad.
Richards was named to the USMNT's 26-man World Cup roster on Tuesday, May 26, less than two weeks after suffering torn ankle ligaments with Crystal Palace.
Advertisement
The 26-year-old has joined his teammates at camp outside Atlanta, where he stayed on Sunday, May 31 as the USMNT defeated Senegal 3-2 in a friendly in Charlotte, North Carolina.
Prior to the game against Senegal, USMNT head coach Mauricio Pochettino admitted he was unsure if Richards would be fit for the World Cup.
“We need to see,” Pochettino said Saturday. “The next few days are going to be key, to see the possibility to be ready or not on the World Cup.”
Richards is a vital part of the U.S. back line, with the Crystal Palace man a locked-in starter at a position where the team has very little depth.
Advertisement
The USMNT faces Germany in Chicago on Saturday, June 6 in its final pre-World Cup friendly.
1 / 26
Who made the USMNT World Cup roster? Meet full 26-man USA squad
The U.S. men's national team World Cup roster is out, for real this time. Three days after the team's squad was fully leaked, USMNT head coach Mauricio Pochettino officially revealed his 26-man roster for the 2026 tournament on Tuesday, May 26. The release confirmed the leak was, in fact, accurate, as the 26 names matched the 26 The Guardian first reported over the weekend. The names didn't feature too many surprises, though there were still one or two decisions that could be characterized as head-scratching. "We are confident this is the best group of 26 players to help us achieve success at the World Cup," Pochettino said. See who made the team:
Christian Pulisic, attacking midfielder/winger
(Kevin C. Cox, Getty Images)
2 / 26
Who made the USMNT World Cup roster? Meet full 26-man USA squad

(Kevin C. Cox, Getty Images)

Who made the USMNT World Cup roster? Meet full 26-man USA squad
Cristian Roldan, midfielder
(Kevin C. Cox, Getty Images)
What are World Cup injury rules?
Richards making the USMNT's final roster is a positive sign, but it still isn't a sure bet he'll take part in the tournament.
FIFA's deadline to submit final rosters is Monday, June 1, with the full list of all 48 teams' squads set to be revealed on Tuesday, June 2.
If an outfield player goes down with a serious injury or illness, a team can still replace him no later than 24 hours before its first match.
That means Richards could still be removed from the roster until June 11 and replaced with a player from the USMNT's preliminary squad, as the team kicks off the World Cup on June 12 against Paraguay.
Advertisement
Pochettino could also simply keep Richards on the roster in the hope that his injury will improve, stashing him on the bench until he's ready.
